q: Which best describes the difference between intraspecific and interspecific competition?
options: Intraspecific competition occurs between different species; interspecific occurs within the same species
Intraspecific competition is always more intense than interspecific competition in stable ecosystems
Intraspecific competition occurs among individuals of the same species competing for the same limited resources; interspecific competition occurs between individuals of different species
Intraspecific competition leads to mutualism; interspecific competition always leads to one species going locally extinct
explanation: Intraspecific = within one species (e.g., deer competing for grazing); interspecific = between different species (e.g., deer and sheep competing for the same grass).
